claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists in evidence-dispatch.ts with git-backed and non-git fallback coverage that detects tracked, untracked, and gitignored file mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files output with walkFiles fallback, excludes allowed artifact roots, and creates digests for all project files.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that detects mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get cached, other, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore.
evidence.why: Git-backed detection covers tracked files and respects standard git exclusions.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks the filesystem as fallback for non-git projects, excluding node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache, and .git directories.
evidence.why: Non-git fallback ensures boundary detection works without git repository.

evidence.summary: Combines git output with walkFiles as union: git projects use both; non-git projects use walkFiles only.
evidence.why: Hybrid approach ensures complete coverage; gitignored files are detected via walkFiles complement.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest generates SHA256 hashes for files, stores symlink targets, and captures mode/size/mtime for other nodes.
evidence.why: Digest mechanism enables precise change detection across file types.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots and detects added, modified, and deleted entries.
evidence.why: Change detection logic that identifies boundary violations.

evidence.summary: excludedDir filters .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walks.
evidence.why: Performance optimization that skips known build/cache directories.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations and reports BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Evidence that git untracked file detection works as intended.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects gitignored .env mutations outside excluded dirs and blocks readiness.
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored files are detected via walkFiles complement to git ls-files.

evidence.summary: Test verifies non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores writes within allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback walks correctly exclude artifact directories.

evidence.summary: Test verifies non-git boundary detects added symlink entries to outside targets.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary detects symlink additions in non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch audits project file boundaries before and after; any mutation outside allowed artifact root becomes a blocker.
evidence.why: Design contract that boundary enforcement is a hard requirement.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundaryBefore snapshot, runs agents, builds boundaryAfter snapshot, and diffs to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Production flow that puts boundary snapshots around agent execution.

risks: Union of git ls-files and walkFiles means gitignored files are included in boundary; if policy intended to exclude gitignored files entirely, line 101 logic needs clarification.

result.notes: Implementation exists with comprehensive test coverage. Git-backed detection uses ls-files for tracked/untracked plus walkFiles for gitignored complement. Non-git fallback walks entire filesystem. Boundary snapshot excludes only allowed artifact roots. Stress tests in .yolo/stress show real Claude agent runs. No evidence of missing implementation.
